skill.name: ai-maestro-documentation-search
skill.description: Search AI Maestro documentation before implementation to find function signatures, classes, APIs, comments, and existing project patterns.
skill.summary: Search indexed project documentation before coding changes.
skill.author: 23blocks-OS

security_audit.summary: The scanner reported command, filesystem, network, and blocker patterns. Most command detections are markdown examples. The weak cryptography and reconnaissance detections are false positives. The skill still directs broad proactive Bash execution for any task and references installation into a hidden home directory path. No evidence found of credential theft or external data exfiltration.

high_findings.title: Overbroad Prompt Control for Automatic Tool Use
high_findings.description: The skill says documentation search is mandatory for any user instruction. This is not confirmed malware. It still broadly controls agent behavior and can cause unrequested Bash execution.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The markdown directly uses mandatory language such as automatic behavior, not optional, any instruction, and must first. The risk is behavioral control and tool forcing rather than confirmed malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Bash Command Execution Driven by User Task Terms
medium_findings.description: The skill tells agents to run local documentation search commands from user request terms. Examples mostly quote arguments. Unsafe execution is possible if an agent interpolates terms poorly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: There is clear evidence of Bash commands that use task-derived search terms. The commands appear intended for local documentation lookup, so the finding is moderate rather than definitive command injection.

medium_findings.title: Hidden Home Directory Installation Path
medium_findings.description: The skill references installing helper scripts into ~/.local/bin and checking that hidden home directory path. Users may not expect persistent user-directory changes from a documentation-search skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced hidden directory and install command are explicit. The instructions appear operational, not covert, so the risk is persistence and user consent rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: Localhost API Connectivity Check
low_findings.description: The skill suggests using curl against http://localhost:23000/api/agents to confirm AI Maestro is running. This targets localhost. No evidence found of external exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is explicitly localhost, which strongly limits exfiltration risk. It still requires network access to a local service and should be disclosed.

low_findings.title: Static Blocker Patterns Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The scanner reported weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance patterns. Review found no cryptographic operation and no meaningful reconnaissance behavior at the cited lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptions, tables, and command names, not cryptographic APIs or system inventory commands. No evidence found that these scanner blocker hits represent real high-risk behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Forced Bash-First Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill combines mandatory prompt language with Bash-only tooling. This can cause local commands to run before ordinary user requests are handled.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tool is Bash and the instructions explicitly make command execution the default behavior. The commands are local documentation tools, but the policy scope is too broad.

content.value_statement: Developers often miss existing signatures, APIs, and patterns before editing code. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to query indexed AI Maestro documentation first.
actual_capabilities: Prompts the agent to search indexed documentation before implementation tasks.
Shows commands for semantic search, keyword search, document retrieval, and document listing.
Explains how to find documentation by type, including functions, classes, modules, interfaces, and components.
Includes examples for indexing current or specified projects with documentation tools.
Describes troubleshooting steps for missing scripts, empty indexes, and local API connectivity.
limitations: Requires AI Maestro documentation tools to be installed and available on PATH.
Depends on an existing documentation index and documented source code.
Uses Bash commands, so command review is important before execution.
Does not generate documentation by itself; it searches and indexes available documentation.

use_cases.title: Check Function Signatures
use_cases.description: Look up documented parameters and return behavior before changing code that calls an existing function.

use_cases.title: Find Existing Patterns
use_cases.description: Search class, module, and component documentation to compare a proposed change with documented project conventions.

use_cases.title: Prepare Safer Refactors
use_cases.description: Index and search project documentation before planning service, API, or interface updates across a codebase.

prompt_templates.title: Search Before a Small Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Before editing this function, search the indexed documentation for its name and summarize the relevant signature and usage notes.

prompt_templates.title: Find API Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the documentation for this API or service name, then list the documented inputs, outputs, and important constraints.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Existing Patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for similar documented classes or components, then recommend the pattern that best matches this new implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Documentation Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Check documentation index statistics, list relevant document types, and identify where documentation is missing before implementation begins.

output_examples.input: Search docs for PaymentService before adding a refund method.
output_examples.output: Found class documentation for PaymentService, related payment flow notes, and existing method naming patterns. Recommended matching the documented service interface before editing.

output_examples.input: Look up authentication helpers before changing login validation.
output_examples.output: Found keyword matches for authentication and validateUser. Summarized expected parameters, return behavior, and related controller documentation.

output_examples.input: Check whether the project has indexed documentation.
output_examples.output: Reported index statistics, listed available document types, and suggested re-indexing when no relevant documents were found.

best_practices: Review each Bash command before execution, especially when a query contains user-provided text.
Index documentation after major code changes so search results remain accurate.
Use keyword search for exact names and semantic search for broader concepts.
anti_patterns: Do not run installation commands without user approval and a clear explanation.
Do not treat missing documentation as proof that a function or API does not exist.
Do not paste untrusted user text into shell commands without proper quoting.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides the agent to search AI Maestro documentation before coding or refactoring work.

faq.question: Does it require AI Maestro?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented commands assume AI Maestro documentation tools and a local documentation index.

faq.question: Can it search exact function names?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes keyword search examples for exact names such as functions, classes, and controllers.

faq.question: Can it index a project?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes commands for indexing the current project or a specific project path.

faq.question: Why is the security risk high?
faq.answer: The skill uses mandatory instructions that push Bash command execution for every task, including commands based on user terms.

faq.question: Is external data exfiltration confirmed?
faq.answer: No. The only URL found is a localhost API check, and no evidence found of external data exfiltration.
